recalls.Description: The AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toy has been recalled due to a serious choking hazard. This toy features an off-white disc shape with a red or blue ball at its center, accompanied by six colorful pull string 'tentacles' and three free-spinning rings. The model number '688-59' is indicated on the packaging, highlighting its identification.
recalls.Title: AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toy Recall Due to Choking Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Vanfun via email at recallmls@outlook.com for assistance.

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of 15 choking incidents and two incidents of children able to bite pieces off the silicone strings.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2022 to September 2025 for approximately $10.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Mailesi Technology Co., Ltd., dba Vanfun, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toy immediately and contact Vanfun for a full refund. Cut and discard all string tentacles, mark the main body 'DESTROYED' with a permanent marker, and email a photo of the destroyed toy to recallmls@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of AiTuiTui Pull String Teething Toy due to choking risk. Contact Vanfun for refund and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: The Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy has been recalled due to safety concerns related to choking. This toy features an off-white disc with a red or blue ball at its center and six colorful silicone pull string tentacles. It also includes three spinning rings and soft buttons on the disc, with the model number 688-59 displayed on the packaging. The design does not meet safety standards, as the silicone strings can pose a serious choking risk.
recalls.Title: Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy Recall Due to Choking Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Longyanguiheng via email at yetonamr_recall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – blue
Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – blue
Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – red
Recalled Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy – red

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of 32 choking incidents.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from June 2025 to October 2025, priced between $10 and $16.

Importers.Name: Longyan Xinluo District Guiheng E-Commerce Co. Ltd., dba Longyanguiheng, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to silicone strings being smaller than allowed, which can obstruct the airway.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the teething toy immediately, remove it from children, and contact Longyanguiheng for a full refund. Cut and discard all silicone tentacles, mark the toy as 'DESTROYED', and email a photo of the destroyed item to yetonamr_recall@163.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Yetonamr Pull String Teething Toy due to choking hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Chews Life is recalling its Silicone Rosary teethers, which include the Assisi, Little Flower, God's Grace, Cecilia, John the Baptist, and Divine Mercy models. Additionally, the recall affects the Silicone Decade teethers, featuring the Genesis, Maccabees, Ester, Wisdom, Divine Mercy, Psalm, and Proverbs models. These teethers are characterized by multi-colored beads and a cross at the end, equipped with a two-piece safety snap clasp.
recalls.Title: Chews Life Recalls Silicone Rosary and Decade Teethers Due to Choking Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Chews Life at 888-742-2276 from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email sandy@chewslife.com. More information is available at https://chewslife.com/pages/clasp-recall or by visiting www.chewslife.com and selecting 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Replacement safety snap clasp on repaired teethers

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 92 reports of the safety snap clasp detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at chewslife.com from January 2024 to April 2024, priced between $20 and $38.

Distributors.Name: Chews Life, of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Detachable part of the safety snap clasp may pose a choking risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled teethers and contact Chews Life for a free repair. A prepaid shipping label will be provided to return the items, and repaired teethers will be sent back at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: Chews Life recalls Silicone Rosary and Decade teethers due to choking hazards. Contact for free repairs and more information.
